natural_occurrence.description: A naturally occurring variant of vitamin E derived primarily from soybean oil. Acts as a natural antioxidant with unique protective properties.

manufacturing.description: Extracted from soybean oil deodorizer distillate and other vegetable oil processing by-products. Often co-extracted with gamma-tocopherol from the same sources. Molecular formula: C27H46O2, Molecular weight: 402.65.

other_uses.pharmaceutical: Component of mixed tocopherol vitamin E supplements

eu.conditions: Evaluated together with E306, E307, E308

usa.notes: Approved as antioxidant and nutrient supplement

regulatory_history.event: EFSA concludes no safety concern at reported uses and levels

adi_context.source_note: EFSA concluded no safety concern at reported uses and levels. Included in group ADI with other tocopherols. Essential nutrient form of vitamin E.

fun_facts.title: Soybean Sidekick
fun_facts.description: Often co-occurs with gamma-tocopherol in soybeans and is co-extracted during oil processing - they're natural partners from the same plant source.

fun_facts.title: Strong but Scarce
fun_facts.description: Has potent antioxidant activity but is less abundant in foods than alpha and gamma forms, making it the 'rare but powerful' member of the tocopherol family.

fun_facts.title: Best in Teams
fun_facts.description: Usually found in mixed tocopherol preparations rather than isolated, as the combination of alpha, gamma, and delta provides synergistic antioxidant protection.

fun_facts.title: Cell Guardian
fun_facts.description: Studies show it helps protect cells from oxidative stress and can help regulate gene expression - it's not just a preservative, it's actively protective at the cellular level.

fun_facts.title: Research Interest
fun_facts.description: Increasingly studied for unique anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties that may differ from other tocopherol forms, though research is still emerging.
